name: Balendra Shah
biography: Balendra Shah (widely known as Balen Shah) is the president of the Rastriya Swatantra Party (RSP) and the party's prime ministerial candidate for the 2026 general election. Born in Kathmandu on April 27, 1990, he trained as a civil engineer before rising to fame as one of Nepal's most prominent hip-hop artists, known for lyrics addressing corruption and inequality. He served as the 15th Mayor of Kathmandu from 2022, resigning in January 2026 to contest the general election. He defeated former four-time Prime Minister KP Sharma Oli by a historic margin of 49,614 votes in the Jhapa-5 constituency.

majorContributions.title: Kathmandu Urban Renewal
majorContributions.description: As Mayor of Kathmandu (2022–2026), led large-scale encroachment removal and river corridor reclamation along the Bagmati, Bishnumati, and Dhobikhola rivers.

majorContributions.title: Anti-Corruption Drive
majorContributions.description: Spearheaded accountability campaigns in Kathmandu Metropolitan City, refusing politically influenced contracts and streamlining revenue collection.

majorContributions.title: Youth Political Mobilization
majorContributions.description: Through his music and political career, mobilized a generation of young Nepalis into civic participation, becoming a symbol of the Gen Z revolution in Nepali politics.

sources.label: Kathmandu Post – Poised to become PM

name: Indira Rana Magar
biography: Indira Rana Magar is an RSP politician who achieved one of the highest vote totals in the 2026 election, winning Jhapa-2 with 60,110 votes against CPN-UML's Devaraj Ghimire.

name: Sobita Gautam
biography: Sobita Gautam is a prominent leader of the Rastriya Swatantra Party who won the Chitwan-3 constituency in the 2026 general election with 59,277 votes, defeating Renu Dahal of NCP by a margin of 38,662 votes.

majorContributions.title: Women's Political Leadership
majorContributions.description: One of the senior female leaders of RSP, advancing women's representation in Nepal's political landscape.

name: Lekh Jung Thapa
biography: Dr. Lekh Jung Thapa of RSP won the Rupandehi-3 parliamentary seat by a margin of 50,459 votes — possibly the largest victory margin in Nepal's parliamentary election history. He secured 58,894 votes, leaving runner-up Sushil Gurung of Nepali Congress with just 8,435 votes. All 35 other candidates lost their election deposits by failing to reach the 10% threshold.

sources.label: Clickmandu – Lekh Jung Thapa wins by record margin
Kathmandu Post – All others forfeit deposit

name: Hari Dhakal

biography: Lal Bikram Thapa is a Rastriya Swatantra Party politician elected from Sunsari-2 in the 2026 Nepal general election with 57,348 votes, defeating Rajib Koirala of Nepali Congress.

name: Sulav Kharel
biography: Sulav Kharel is an RSP politician who won Rupandehi-2 with 56,550 votes, defeating former CPN-UML vice-chair and Finance Minister Bishnu Prasad Paudel by a margin of 43,689 votes.

name: Rubina Acharya
biography: Rubina Acharya is one of Nepal's most beloved comedians and actresses, widely known by her stage name. She joined RSP and won the Morang-6 constituency with 55,513 votes, defeating former Nepali Congress leader Dr. Shekhar Koirala. Her win represents RSP's ability to draw prominent cultural figures into its fold.

majorContributions.title: Cultural and Entertainment Leadership
majorContributions.description: A prominent figure in Nepali comedy and entertainment for over a decade, bringing a popular and grassroots perspective to politics.

name: Sunil Lamsal
biography: Sunil Lamsal is a Rastriya Swatantra Party politician elected from Rupandehi-1 in the 2026 Nepal general election with 54,845 votes, defeating Hira Bahadur Khatri of Nepali Congress.

name: Rabi Lamichhane
biography: Rabi Lamichhane is the chairperson of the Rastriya Swatantra Party (RSP), which he founded in 2022. A former investigative TV journalist known for his show 'Sidha Kura Janata Sanga', he became a political phenomenon after winning Chitwan-2. He served briefly as Deputy Prime Minister and Home Minister in 2022–23. He returned to politics and won Chitwan-2 again in 2026 with a huge margin.

education.degree: Masters in Journalism and Mass Communication

majorContributions.title: Investigative Journalism
majorContributions.description: Ran 'Sidha Kura Janata Sanga', one of Nepal's most watched TV programs, exposing corruption and holding public figures accountable.

majorContributions.title: RSP Founding
majorContributions.description: Founded the Rastriya Swatantra Party in 2022, which went on to win the 2026 general election in a historic landslide.

previousPositions: Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Home Affairs (December 2022 – January 2023)

name: Swarnim Wagle
biography: Dr. Swarnim Wagle is a prominent Nepali economist and RSP Vice-Chair. Educated at Budhanilkantha School, LSE (BSc Economics), Harvard Kennedy School (MPA/ID), and ANU (PhD Economics), he has worked at the World Bank and UNDP and served as Vice-Chairman of Nepal's National Planning Commission (2016–2017). He won the Tanahun-1 constituency in the 2026 election with 38,040 votes.

majorContributions.title: Economic Policy Reform
majorContributions.description: Contributed to Nepal's macroeconomic policy as Vice-Chairman of the National Planning Commission and as a prolific policy writer.

majorContributions.title: International Development Work
majorContributions.description: Worked at the World Bank and UNDP on economic development programs for Nepal and the broader South Asian region.

previousPositions: Vice-Chairman, National Planning Commission of Nepal (2016–2017)

sources.label: Nepal Press – Swarnim Wagle wins Tanahun-1

sources.label: RSP - Bharat Prasad Parajuli candidate page

name: Janak Singh Dhami
biography: Janak Singh Dhami is an RSP politician who won Kanchanpur-1 with 24,332 votes by a margin of 13,222 over an NCP candidate.

name: Buddha Ratna Maharjan
biography: Buddha Ratna Maharjan is an RSP politician who won Lalitpur-1 with 23,373 votes, defeating Udaya Shumsher Rana of Nepali Congress.

name: Bikram Timilsina

name: KP Khanal
biography: KP Khanal is a Rastriya Swatantra Party politician elected from Kailali-2 in the 2026 Nepal general election with 19,057 votes, defeating Pati Ram Chaudhary of Nagarik Unmukti Party, Nepal.

name: Rajunath Pandey
biography: Rajunath Pandey is an RSP politician who won Kathmandu-3 with 18,757 votes, defeating Kulman Ghising.

biography: Ashish Gajurel is a Rastriya Swatantra Party politician elected from Sindhuli-2 in the 2026 Nepal general election with 17,940 votes, defeating Lekh Nath Dahal of Nepali Communist Party.

biography: Govinda Panthi is a Rastriya Swatantra Party politician elected from Gulmi-2 in the 2026 Nepal general election with 16,967 votes, defeating Gokarna Raj Bista of CPN-UML.

name: Dhanendra Karki
biography: Dhanendra Karki is a Rastriya Swatantra Party politician elected from Sindhuli-1 in the 2026 Nepal general election with 16,659 votes, defeating Pradip Kumar Katuwal Chhetri of CPN-UML.

name: Ranju Darshana
biography: Ranju Darshana (Ranju Neupane) is an RSP politician who won Kathmandu-1 with 15,455 votes, defeating Prabal Thapa of Nepali Congress.

biography: Surya Bahadur Lama is an RSP politician who won Udayapur-2 with 14,802 votes, defeating Ambar Bahadur Rayamajhi of CPN-UML.
